name: Carrasqueta and Torremanzanas Loop from Xixona
name_original: El Puerto de la Carrasqueta — Circular desde Jijona por Torremanzanas
description: This difficult 27.8-mile mountain biking route from Jijona to Torremanzanas offers challenging terrain and stunning Mediterranean views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Xixona / Jijona offers diverse terrain, characterized by spectacular mountain scenery and varied natural landscapes. The region features significant elevations like the Sierra de Aitana and Maigmó Summit, providing challenging climbs and descents. Riders can explore areas with dense Mediterranean forests, deep ravines, and panoramic viewpoints. The network of trails caters to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult. Best mountain bike trails around Xixona / Jijona The most popular mountain bike trail is Carrasqueta and Torremanzanas Loop from Xixona, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 19 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging terrain and Mediterranean views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is L'Estepar Rest Area – Sant Antoni Tunnel loop from l'Almoraig, a moderate 29.0 miles (46.7 km) path. It winds through areas with pine and holm oak forests. Local mountain bikers also love the Carrasqueta Viewpoint – Carrasqueta Pass loop from Xixona, a 22.1 miles (35.7 km) trail leading through the Carrasqueta Pass, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Xixona / Jijona is defined by mountain passes, Mediterranean forests, and panoramic viewpoints. The trail network offers options for moderate to difficult rides, with some sections requiring advanced skills. The routes in Xixona / Jijona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 1600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Xixona / Jijona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Xixona / Jijona?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails around Xixona / Jijona, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Xixona / Jijona?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, characterized by spectacular mountain scenery and varied natural landscapes. You'll encounter significant elevations like the Sierra de Aitana and Maigmó Summit, providing challenging climbs and descents. Trails often wind through dense Mediterranean forests, deep ravines, and past panoramic viewpoints. Some sections may require advanced riding skills or even bike pushing.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Xixona / Jijona?
A: While many trails are rated moderate to difficult, the region does offer some easier options suitable for all skill levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Estepar Rest Area – Sant Antoni Tunnel loop from l'Almoraig&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 46.7 km path that winds through pine and holm oak forests, offering a less strenuous ride compared to the more challenging routes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking in Xixona / Jijona?
A: You'll find several impressive landmarks and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2604644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views of pine and holm oak forests and the distant coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó Summit&lt;/a&gt; provides exceptional panoramic views stretching from Benidorm to Torrevieja. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2874564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante Balcony&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas of Alicante city and the Costa Blanca coastline.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Xixona / Jijona are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927474501/carrasqueta-and-torremanzanas-loop-from-xixona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta and Torremanzanas Loop from Xixona&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 44.7 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33917112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Viewpoint – Carrasqueta Pass loop from Xixona&lt;/a&gt; is a 35.7 km trail through the Carrasqueta Pass.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Xixona / Jijona?
A: The mountain bike trails in Xixona / Jijona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 140 reviews. Riders often praise the spectacular mountain scenery, the diverse natural landscapes, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs and descents. The panoramic views from points like the Carrasqueta Pass and Maigmó Summit are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer a good challenge for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927474501/carrasqueta-and-torremanzanas-loop-from-xixona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta and Torremanzanas Loop from Xixona&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.7 km trail with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10352374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass – La Sarga Rock Paintings loop from Xixona&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 43.3 km route that takes around 4 hours 10 minutes to complete.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the mountain bike routes?
A: You'll encounter a rich variety of natural features. The region is known for its pine and holm oak forests, especially around the Carrasqueta Pass and in areas like El Carrascal de la Font Roja Natural Park. You might also spot impressive rock cliffs, deep ravines, and unique natural swimming holes like Salt de Jijona. The protected forest ecosystem around Maigmó Summit is rich in native Mediterranean flora.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through historical or cultural sites?
A: While primarily focused on natural landscapes, some routes may pass near historical remnants. For example, trails in El Carrascal de la Font Roja Natural Park wind past old country houses and ice houses. The region also features 18th-century snow caves ('Neveras') in the Sierra de Aitana. Additionally, the town of Jijona itself has a medieval old town and the Castillo de Jijona (Torre Grossa).

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Xixona / Jijona?
A: The region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months. Winters are generally mild, allowing for year-round activity, though higher elevations might experience cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any specific trails that lead to unique natural attractions like waterfalls or caves?
A: While not explicitly waterfalls, the region features unique natural water formations like the Salt de Jijona, a natural swimming hole carved into rock, which offers a refreshing break. Some routes, particularly those around Cabeçó d'Or, can lead cyclists towards historic caves like Cuevas de Canelobre, offering a chance to explore beyond the trails.
tips: Long and quiet port that climbs up to 1020 meters, with the possibility of continuing to climb with a gravel. Going down it from Jijona is brutal, many curves and incredible views over the city of Alicante and the sea.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the El Puerto de la Carrasqueta — Circular desde Jijona mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult and is best suited for experienced mountain bikers with very good physical condition. Some sections may be quite technical, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route usually takes around 4 hours and 20 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. While the main climb to La Carrasqueta often features good asphalt, the circular route incorporates more technical off-road elements, including potential singletrack, gravel, and possibly rocky sections. Be aware that some parts can be exposed with little tree cover.

faqs.question: What are the views like along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning panoramic views, especially over the city of Alicante and the Mediterranean Sea, particularly on the descent towards Jijona. The route also passes through picturesque landscapes of almond and olive fields.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks will I encounter?
faqs.answer: A major highlight is the iconic Carrasqueta Pass, a famous cycling climb. The starting point, Jijona, is known for its traditional turrón. You'll also enjoy consistent panoramic views throughout the ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Jijona, you can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The ideal times to ride this route are during spring and autumn. Summers can be very hot and exposed, while winters might bring cold and strong winds, making the ride less enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is the El Puerto de la Carrasqueta — Circular desde Jijona mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: There are no specific restrictions mentioned regarding dogs on this route. However, given the route's difficulty, varied terrain, and potential exposure, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness and paw pads. Always keep your dog under control and follow local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access this mountain bike route, as it uses public trails and roads.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. You will encounter sections of the Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm, [CIMA AT02] La Carrasqueta, El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and Camí de la Torre de les Maçanes.

faqs.question: Are there any specific times to avoid for a more peaceful ride?
faqs.answer: The winding roads of La Carrasqueta are popular with motorcyclists, especially on weekends. For a quieter experience, consider riding on weekdays.
